commit_scope: Compile BML hexadecimal and binary integer source literals into BMA-native execution with forward/backward proof.

local_validation.notes: The numeric literal source proof returns 1073741823 across Go/Rust/TypeScript kernels. Existing scanner and source-to-BMA regression proofs remain green: bml-thesis-file-execution-proof returns 131071 and bml-thesis-control-primitives-source-proof returns 262143. The kernel-native audit reports no new unexpected BML/BMF host-native semantics.

ci_validation.notes: Pending push, PR, and GitHub checks.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: A .bml source file containing thesis-style hexadecimal and binary integer literals now parses through native and Form BML scanners, lowers into BMA return-int programs, executes forward to numeric values, and replays backward to the empty VM state.

phase_gate.reason: This closes BML source-to-native BMA support for hexadecimal and binary integer literals. Full BML thesis parity remains open: float literals, character escape parity, arbitrary expression/control lowering, and whole companion-corpus native execution are still required.

evidence_refs: form/form-stdlib/tests/fixtures/bml-thesis-numeric-literals.bml is the source fixture for the proof.
form/form-stdlib/tests/bml-thesis-numeric-literals-source-proof.fk proves native file scanning, Form text scanning, BMF rule parse shape, BMA return values, and backward restoration.
docs/system_audit/bml_thesis_feature_audit_20260601.md records the newly proven hex/bin source forms and keeps remaining full-parity gaps explicit.
